1.o: Jesus Christ is a Christian's hope; all our hopes of eternal life are built upon him; and Christ is in us the hope of glory. The apostle seems to have been the means of Timothy's conversion; who served with him in his ministry, as a dutiful son with a loving father. That which raises questions, is not for edifying; that which gives occasion for doubtful disputes, pulls down the church rather than builds it up. Godliness of heart and life can only be kept up and increased, by the exercise of faith in the truths and promises of God, through Jesus Christ.
1.m:
Jesus Christ is a Christian's hope. All our hopes for eternal life depend on him. And Christ inside us gives us hope for glory.

The apostle was likely the one who led Timothy to believe in Christ. Timothy worked alongside him, just like a good son works with a caring father.

Things that cause confusion do not help build up the church. They tear it down instead. True godliness grows only when we trust in God’s truths and promises through Jesus Christ.

1.o: Whatever tends to weaken love to God, or love to the brethren, tends to defeat the end of the commandment. The design of the gospel is answered, when sinners, through repentance towards God and faith in Jesus Christ, are brought to exercise Christian love. And as believers were righteous persons in God's appointed way, the law was not against them. But unless we are made righteous by faith in Christ, really repenting and forsaking sin, we are yet under the curse of the law, even according to the gospel of the blessed God, and are unfit to share the holy happiness of heaven.
1.m:
Anything that weakens our love for God or for others goes against what the commandments are meant to do. The goal of the gospel is to help sinners turn away from their sins and believe in Jesus Christ. This leads them to show love to other Christians. 

Believers become righteous people in God’s eyes because of their faith. But if we don’t truly repent and stop sinning, we remain under the punishment of the law. Even in the good news about Jesus, those who haven’t turned from their sins can't join the happy life of heaven.

1.o: The apostle knew that he would justly have perished, if the Lord had been extreme to mark what was amiss; and also if his grace and mercy had not been abundant to him when dead in sin, working faith and love to Christ in his heart. This is a faithful saying; these are true and faithful words, which may be depended on, That the Son of God came into the world, willingly and purposely to save sinners. No man, with Paul's example before him, can question the love and power of Christ to save him, if he really desires to trust in him as the Son of God, who once died on the cross, and now reigns upon the throne of glory, to save all that come to God through him. Let us then admire and praise the grace of God our Saviour; and ascribe to the Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, three Persons in the unity of the Godhead, the glory of all done in, by, and for us.
1.m:
The apostle knew that he would have deserved punishment if God had focused on his mistakes. But God’s grace and mercy were very generous toward him while he was still lost in sin, creating faith and love for Christ in his heart.

This is true and dependable: The Son of God came into the world to save sinners willingly and purposefully. With Paul’s example, no one can doubt that Jesus loves and has the power to save anyone who truly trusts in him—the one who died on the cross and now rules from heaven to help those who turn to God through him.

Let us then praise the grace of God our Savior. We should give honor to the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit—three persons united as one God—for everything done for us.

1.o: The ministry is a warfare against sin and Satan; carried on under the Lord Jesus, who is the Captain of our salvation. The good hopes others have had of us, should stir us up to duty. And let us be upright in our conduct in all things. The design of the highest censures in the primitive church, was, to prevent further sin, and to reclaim the sinner. May all who are tempted to put away a good conscience, and to abuse the gospel, remember that this is the way to make shipwreck of faith also.
1.m:
Serving God means fighting against sin and Satan. We do this under the leadership of Jesus, who is our Captain. When people expect good things from us, it should motivate us to do our duties well. Let’s always act honestly and fairly.

In the early church, the harshest punishments were meant to stop people from sinning more and help them turn back to the right path. 

Let those who might want to ignore their conscience or misuse the teachings of the gospel remember that doing so will lead to losing their faith completely.

2.o: The disciples of Christ must be praying people; all, without distinction of nation, sect, rank, or party. Our duty as Christians, is summed up in two words; godliness, that is, the right worshipping of God; and honesty, that is, good conduct toward all men. These must go together: we are not truly honest, if we are not godly, and do not render to God his due; and we are not truly godly, if not honest. What is acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour, we should abound in. There is one Mediator, and that Mediator gave himself a ransom for all. And this appointment has been made for the benefit of the Jews and the Gentiles of every nation; that all who are willing may come in this way, to the mercy-seat of a pardoning God, to seek reconciliation with him. Sin had made a quarrel between us and God; Jesus Christ is the Mediator who makes peace. He is a ransom that was to be known in due time. In the Old Testament times, his sufferings, and the glory that should follow, were spoken of as things to be revealed in the last times. Those who are saved must come to the knowledge of the truth, for that is God's appointed way to save sinners: if we do not know the truth, we cannot be ruled by it.
2.m:
Christ’s followers need to pray. Everyone, no matter their country, religion, social status, or group, should pray. As Christians, our main duties are godliness—worshiping God correctly—and honesty—treating everyone well. We can't be truly honest unless we're also godly and give God what he deserves. And we can't be truly godly unless we're honest too. 

We should focus on doing what pleases God our Savior. There is only one mediator, and that mediator paid the price to free everyone from sin. This offer applies to both Jews and people from other nations so anyone who wants to can find forgiveness through Jesus. 

Sin created a problem between us and God. But Jesus came as the mediator to make peace. His sacrifice was meant to be understood at the right time. Even in the old days, people knew about his future suffering and coming victory. To be saved, people must learn the truth because knowing the truth is how God saves sinners. Without knowing the truth, we can’t follow it.

2.o: Under the gospel, prayer is not to be confined to any one particular house of prayer, but men must pray every where. We must pray in our closets, pray in our families, pray at our meals, pray when we are on journeys, and pray in the solemn assemblies, whether more public or private. We must pray in charity; without wrath, or malice, or anger at any person. We must pray in faith, without doubting, and without disputing. Women who profess the Christian religion, must be modest in apparel, not affecting gaudiness, gaiety, or costliness. Good works are the best ornament; these are, in the sight of God, of great price. Modesty and neatness are more to be consulted in garments than elegance and fashion. And it would be well if the professors of serious godliness were wholly free from vanity in dress. They should spend more time and money in relieving the sick and distressed, than in decorating themselves and their children. To do this in a manner unsuitable to their rank in life, and their profession of godliness, is sinful. These are not trifles, but Divine commands. The best ornaments for professors of godliness, are good works. According to St. Paul, women are not allowed to be public teachers in the church; for teaching is an office of authority. But good women may and ought to teach their children at home the principles of true religion. Also, women must not think themselves excused from learning what is necessary to salvation, though they must not usurp authority. As woman was last in the creation, which is one reason for her subjection, so she was first in the transgression. But there is a word of comfort; that those who continue in sobriety, shall be saved in child-bearing, or with child-bearing, by the Messiah, who was born of a woman. And the especial sorrow to which the female sex is subject, should cause men to exercise their authority with much gentleness, tenderness, and affection.
2.m:
When it comes to praying under the new teachings, people shouldn’t limit their prayers to just one place. They need to pray everywhere—alone in their rooms, with family, before meals, while traveling, and during meetings, whether big or small.

People should pray kindly, without being angry or mean toward anyone. They should pray with faith, believing what they’re saying and not questioning it.

Women who follow Jesus should dress simply and modestly. They shouldn’t wear fancy or expensive clothes. Instead, doing good deeds is the best way to look nice. Being neat and tidy is better than following fashions. It’s important for believers to avoid dressing up too much. They should use more of their time and money helping others who are sick or struggling rather than buying fancy clothes for themselves and their kids. Doing things that don’t match their status or beliefs is wrong. These aren’t little details but important rules from God.

The best way for believers to stand out is through their actions. Women shouldn’t try to lead churches publicly because leading is a position of authority. However, women can teach their children about God at home. They still need to learn what’s needed for salvation, even if they shouldn’t take over roles meant for men.

Since Eve was created after Adam, and she led them both into sin, women have been taught to submit. Yet, there is hope. Those who stay sober and faithful will find salvation through Christ, who came from a woman. Because women often suffer sadness, men should treat them gently and with care.

3.o: If a man desired the pastoral office, and from love to Christ, and the souls of men, was ready to deny himself, and undergo hardships by devoting himself to that service, he sought to be employed in a good work, and his desire should be approved, provided he was qualified for the office. A minister must give as little occasion for blame as can be, lest he bring reproach upon his office. He must be sober, temperate, moderate in all his actions, and in the use of all creature-comforts. Sobriety and watchfulness are put together in Scripture, they assist one the other. The families of ministers ought to be examples of good to all other families. We should take heed of pride; it is a sin that turned angels into devils. He must be of good repute among his neighbours, and under no reproach from his former life. To encourage all faithful ministers, we have Christ's gracious word of promise, Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world, Mt 28:20. And he will fit his ministers for their work, and carry them through difficulties with comfort, and reward their faithfulness.
3.m:
If someone wanted to become a pastor because they loved Jesus and cared about people’s souls, and was willing to sacrifice and face challenges to serve God, then their desire should be respected if they were qualified for the job.

A pastor needs to avoid doing anything wrong so that others won’t think badly of the ministry. They need to be sensible and careful in everything they do, especially when enjoying things that can be enjoyed moderately.

Sobriety and being alert go hand in hand. Both help each other. Ministers’ families should set a good example for everyone else. Pride can lead to trouble, just like it did when angels became demons.

Pastors should be well thought of by those around them, and there shouldn’t be anything in their past that would cause shame.

To support all faithful pastors, remember what Jesus promised: “I am with you always, to the very end of the age,” Matthew 28:20. Jesus helps his ministers do their jobs well, guides them through tough times, and rewards their loyalty.

3.o: The deacons were at first appointed to distribute the charity of the church, and to manage its concerns, yet pastors and evangelists were among them. The deacons had a great trust reposed in them. They must be grave, serious, prudent men. It is not fit that public trusts should be lodged in the hands of any, till they are found fit for the business with which they are to be trusted. All who are related to ministers, must take great care to walk as becomes the gospel of Christ.
3.m:
The deacons were chosen to handle the church’s charity and manage its affairs. Pastors and evangelists were part of their group too. 

Deacons had important jobs to do. They needed to be mature, wise, and responsible people. We shouldn’t give big responsibilities to those who aren’t ready for them.

Everyone connected to ministers needs to behave properly, fitting for followers of Jesus.

3.o: The church is the house of God; he dwells there. The church holds forth the Scripture and the doctrine of Christ, as a pillar holds forth a proclamation. When a church ceases to be the pillar and ground of truth, we may and ought to forsake her; for our regard to truth should be first and greatest. The mystery of godliness is Christ. He is God, who was made flesh, and was manifest in the flesh. God was pleased to manifest himself to man, by his own Son taking the nature of man. Though reproached as a sinner, and put to death as a malefactor, Christ was raised again by the Spirit, and so was justified from all the false charges with which he was loaded. Angels ministered to him, for he is the Lord of angels. The Gentiles welcomed the gospel which the Jews rejected. Let us remember that God was manifest in the flesh, to take away our sins, to redeem us from all iniquity, and to purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works. These doctrines must be shown forth by the fruits of the Spirit in our lives.
3.m:
The church is God’s house where he dwells. It teaches the Scriptures and the teachings about Christ, just like a pillar holds up a sign. We should leave a church if it stops being a support for truth. Our love for truth should come first.

Christ is the mystery of godliness. He is God who became human and lived among us. God showed himself to humans through his son Jesus, who took on human form. Even though Jesus was treated badly and killed as a criminal, God raised him back to life, proving he was innocent. Angels served him because he rules over them.

Gentiles accepted the message that the Jews rejected. Remember, God came in human form to forgive our sins, save us from wrongdoing, and make us his special people who do good deeds. This truth must show in how we live our lives.

4.o: The Holy Spirit, both in the Old and the New Testament, spoke of a general turning from the faith of Christ, and the pure worship of God. This should come during the Christian dispensation, for those are called the latter days. False teachers forbid as evil what God has allowed, and command as a duty what he has left indifferent. We find exercise for watchfulness and self-denial, in attending to the requirements of God's law, without being tasked to imaginary duties, which reject what he has allowed. But nothing justifies an intemperate or improper use of things; and nothing will be good to us, unless we seek by prayer for the Lord's blessing upon it.
4.m:
The Holy Spirit talked about people turning away from Jesus' teachings and true worship of God in both old and new parts of the Bible. This would happen later on, after Christianity started. 

False teachers tell people to avoid things God said were okay, and they order them to do things God didn't really care about. Following God’s rules helps us stay alert and strong. It’s important to focus on what God actually asks us to do, not make up our own tasks.

But we shouldn’t misuse anything God allows. And whatever we do needs God’s help to be truly good. So we should pray for his blessings on everything we do.

4.o: Outward acts of self-denial profit little. What will it avail us to mortify the body, if we do not mortify sin? No diligence in mere outward things could be of much use. The gain of godliness lies much in the promise; and the promises to godly people relate partly to the life that now is, but especially to the life which is to come: though we lose for Christ, we shall not lose by him. If Christ be thus the Saviour of all men, then much more will he be the Rewarder of those who seek and serve him; he will provide well for those whom he has made new creatures.
4.m:
Acts of self-denial don't help much if you don't get rid of your sins. It doesn’t matter much if you hurt your body but keep sinning. Just doing outward things won’t help much either. 

The real benefit from being godly comes mostly from God’s promises. These promises apply both to our lives here on earth and even more so to our future life after death. Even if we give up some things for Christ, we won’t really lose out because of him.

If Jesus saves everyone, imagine how much he rewards those who truly follow him! He takes good care of those he changes into new people.

4.o: Men's youth will not be despised, if they keep from vanities and follies. Those who teach by their doctrine, must teach by their life. Their discourse must be edifying; their conversation must be holy; they must be examples of love to God and all good men, examples of spiritual-mindedness. Ministers must mind these things as their principal work and business. By this means their profiting will appear in all things, as well as to all persons; this is the way to profit in knowledge and grace, and also to profit others. The doctrine of a minister of Christ must be scriptural, clear, evangelical, and practical; well stated, explained, defended, and applied. But these duties leave no leisure for wordly pleasures, trifling visits, or idle conversation, and but little for what is mere amusement, and only ornamental. May every believer be enabled to let his profiting appear unto all men; seeking to experience the power of the gospel in his own soul, and to bring forth its fruits in his life.
4.m: Young people won't be looked down upon if they avoid silly behaviors. Teachers need to set an example through their actions. They should speak words that build up others and live lives that show love to God and everyone else. They must demonstrate spiritual thinking too. These qualities should be the main focus of ministers. When they do this, their growth in faith will be obvious to everyone. This approach helps them grow in knowledge and grace, and also benefits those around them. A minister’s teachings must come straight from the Bible, be clear, full of truth, and useful. It needs to be presented well, explained clearly, defended strongly, and applied properly. However, these responsibilities don’t allow time for worldly entertainment, small talk, or unimportant activities. There isn’t much room left over for just fun stuff either. Let each believer show their growth in faith to everyone. They should feel the power of the gospel in their hearts and reflect it in their daily lives.

5.o: Respect must be paid to the dignity of years and place. The younger, if faulty, must be rebuked, not as desirous to find fault with them, but as willing to make the best of them. There is need of much meekness and care in reproving those who deserve reproof.
5.m: We should respect older people and those in important positions. If someone younger makes a mistake, we should correct them kindly. We want to help them improve, not criticize them. It takes patience and care to tell someone what they did wrong.

5.o: Honour widows that are widows indeed, relieve them, and maintain them. It is the duty of children, if their parents are in need, and they are able to relieve them, to do it to the utmost of their power. Widowhood is a desolate state; but let widows trust in the Lord, and continue in prayer. All who live in pleasure, are dead while they live, spiritually dead, dead in trespasses and sins. Alas, what numbers there are of this description among nominal Christians, even to the latest period of life! If any men or women do not maintain their poor relations, they in effect deny the faith. If they spend upon their lusts and pleasures, what should maintain their families, they have denied the faith, and are worse than infidels. If professors of the gospel give way to any corrupt principle or conduct, they are worse than those who do not profess to believe the doctrines of grace.
5.m: Honor real widows by helping them and taking care of them. Children should help their needy parents if they can. Being a widow can be hard, so widows should trust God and keep praying. Many people pretend to follow Christ but live selfishly, which makes them spiritually dead. Sadly, many older believers still act this way. People who don’t support their poor family members are denying the faith. Those who waste money on sinful pleasures instead of caring for their families are denying the faith and are worse than non-believers. Anyone who claims to believe in Jesus but acts sinfully is worse than someone who doesn’t claim to believe at all.

5.o: Every one brought into any office in the church, should be free from just censure; and many are proper objects of charity, yet ought not to be employed in public services. Those who would find mercy when they are in distress, must show mercy when they are in prosperity; and those who show most readiness for every good work, are most likely to be faithful in whatever is trusted to them. Those who are idle, very seldom are only idle, they make mischief among neighbours, and sow discord among brethren. All believers are required to relieve those belonging to their families who are destitute, that the church may not be prevented from relieving such as are entirely destitute and friendless.
5.m:
Everyone put in charge in the church should have a clean record. Some people need help, but shouldn’t lead public groups. 

Those who want mercy when they’re down should give mercy when they’re up. People who do lots of good things are usually reliable with bigger tasks too.

Lazy people often cause trouble and spread gossip. Believers should take care of family members who need help so the church can focus on helping others without support.

5.o: Care must be taken that ministers are maintained. And those who are laborious in this work are worthy of double honour and esteem. It is their just due, as much as the reward of the labourer. The apostle charges Timothy solemnly to guard against partiality. We have great need to watch at all times, that we do not partake of other men's sins. Keep thyself pure, not only from doing the like thyself, but from countenancing it, or any way helping to it in others. The apostle also charges Timothy to take care of his health. As we are not to make our bodies masters, so neither slaves; but to use them so that they may be most helpful to us in the service of God. There are secret, and there are open sins: some men's sins are open before-hand, and going before unto judgment; some they follow after. God will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and make known the counsels of all hearts. Looking forward to the judgment-day, let us all attend to our proper offices, whether in higher or lower stations, studying that the name and doctrine of God may never be blasphemed on our account.
5.m:
We need to make sure ministers are supported. Those who work hard deserve extra honor and respect. This is what they should receive, just like workers earn for their labor.

The apostle Paul tells Timothy to be careful about being fair. We always need to watch out so we don’t get involved in other people’s wrongdoings. Stay clean by avoiding both doing bad things yourself and helping others do them.

Paul also tells Timothy to look after his health. We shouldn't let our bodies control us, nor treat them poorly. Instead, we should take good care of ourselves so we can serve God better.

There are hidden sins and obvious ones. Some people commit sins openly, leading to punishment. Others hide their sins, but God will reveal everything. He’ll show everyone’s secrets and intentions.

As we think about the day of judgment, each person should focus on their role—whether high or low. Let’s make sure God’s name and teachings aren’t disrespected because of us.

6.o: Christians were not to suppose that religious knowledge, or Christian privileges, gave them any right to despise heathen masters, or to disobey lawful commands, or to expose their faults to others. And such as enjoyed the privilege of living with believing masters, were not to withhold due respect and reverence, because they were equal in respect to religious privileges, but were to serve with double diligence and cheerfulness, because of their faith in Christ, and as partakers of his free salvation. We are not to consent to any words as wholesome, except the words of our Lord Jesus Christ; to these we must give unfeigned consent. Commonly those are most proud who know least; for they do not know themselves. Hence come envy, strife, railings, evil-surmisings, disputes that are all subtlety, and of no solidity, between men of corrupt and carnal minds, ignorant of the truth and its sanctifying power, and seeking their worldly advantage. (1Ti 6:6-10)
6.m:
Christians should not think that knowing about God gives them the right to look down on non-Christian bosses or to ignore their orders. They shouldn’t tell everyone about their mistakes either.

If you have a believing boss, don't be lazy just because you share the same beliefs. Work even harder and be cheerful because your boss has put faith in Christ. This means you both share in God’s gift of freedom through Jesus.

We should only agree with what Jesus taught. He is the one we must follow without question.

Usually, people who know very little about things tend to be the proudest. They don’t understand themselves well. So, they get jealous, argue, insult each other, and spread rumors. They waste time arguing over silly things instead of focusing on true knowledge and God’s goodness. These people care more about getting ahead in the world than understanding real truth.

6.o: Those that make a trade of Christianity to serve their turn for this world, will be disappointed; but those who mind it as their calling, will find it has the promise of the life that now is, as well as of that which is to come. He that is godly, is sure to be happy in another world; and if contented with his condition in this world, he has enough; and all truly godly people are content. When brought into the greatest straits, we cannot be poorer than when we came into this world; a shroud, a coffin, and a grave, are all that the richest man in the world can have from all his wealth. If nature should be content with a little, grace should be content with less. The necessaries of life bound a true Christian's desires, and with these he will endeavour to be content. We see here the evil of covetousness. It is not said, they that are rich, but they will be rich; who place their happiness in wealth, and are eager and determined in the pursuit. Those that are such, give to Satan the opportunity of tempting them, leading them to use dishonest means, and other bad practices, to add to their gains. Also, leading into so many employments, and such a hurry of business, as leave no time or inclination for spiritual religion; leading to connexions that draw into sin and folly. What sins will not men be drawn into by the love of money! People may have money, and yet not love it; but if they love it, this will push them on to all evil. Every sort of wickedness and vice, in one way or another, grows from the love of money. We cannot look around without perceiving many proofs of this, especially in a day of outward prosperity, great expenses, and loose profession.
6.m:
People who try to use Christianity just to get ahead in this world will be let down. But those who take it seriously as their main focus will find it brings both blessings now and later. A godly person is sure to be happy in heaven. And if they're okay with what they have in this life, they'll be fine. Even when things get really tough, we can't end up poorer than when we were born—just a burial cloth, coffin, and grave are all anyone needs at the end. 

If our natural desire is for a bit of comfort, then having faith should mean being satisfied with even less. True Christians keep their wants within basic necessities and aim to be content with those. Here we see the problem with greed. It’s not about being rich, but wanting to be rich. People who think happiness comes from wealth and chase after it eagerly set themselves up for trouble. They open doors for Satan to tempt them into using unfair ways to get more money. This kind of drive also leaves no room for real spiritual living, pulling them into busy work and harmful connections. 

How many wrongs do greedy people commit? Having money isn’t bad, but loving it too much leads to all sorts of sins. Greed fuels every type of wrongdoing. Just look around during times of plenty and you’ll see examples everywhere—big spending, empty talk, and lots of sinful behavior.

6.o: It ill becomes any men, but especially men of God, to set their hearts upon the things of this world; men of God should be taken up with the things of God. There must be a conflict with corruption, and temptations, and the powers of darkness. Eternal life is the crown proposed for our encouragement. We are called to lay hold thereon. To the rich must especially be pointed out their dangers and duties, as to the proper use of wealth. But who can give such a charge, that is not himself above the love of things that wealth can buy? The appearing of Christ is certain, but it is not for us to know the time. Mortal eyes cannot bear the brightness of the Divine glory. None can approach him except as he is made known unto sinners in and by Christ. The Godhead is here adored without distinction of Persons, as all these things are properly spoken, whether of the Father, the Son, or the Holy Ghost. God is revealed to us, only in and through the human nature of Christ, as the only begotten Son of the Father.
6.m:
It doesn't suit anyone, especially those who follow God, to focus too much on worldly things. People of faith should concentrate on spiritual matters instead. They need to fight against selfish desires, temptations, and evil forces. Eternal life is what we’re working towards. We must strive to achieve it. 

Rich people especially need to be reminded about their responsibilities and the risks of loving money too much. Who can warn others about the dangers of wealth if they themselves are attached to material possessions?

The return of Jesus is sure, though we don’t know when it will happen. Human eyes can’t handle the brilliance of God’s glory. Only those whom God reveals through Christ can approach Him. When we praise God, we honor all three persons of the Trinity—Father, Son, and Holy Spirit—as one. We only see God clearly through Jesus, His one and only Son.

6.o: Being rich in this world is wholly different from being rich towards God. Nothing is more uncertain than worldly wealth. Those who are rich, must see that God gives them their riches; and he only can give to enjoy them richly; for many have riches, but enjoy them poorly, not having a heart to use them. What is the best estate worth, more than as it gives opportunity of doing the more good? Showing faith in Christ by fruits of love, let us lay hold on eternal life, when the self-indulgent, covetous, and ungodly around, lift up their eyes in torment. That learning which opposes the truth of the gospel, is not true science, or real knowledge, or it would approve the gospel, and consent to it. Those who advance reason above faith, are in danger of leaving faith. Grace includes all that is good, and grace is an earnest, a beginning of glory; wherever God gives grace, he will give glory.
6.m:
Being rich in this world is very different from being rich toward God. Worldly wealth is extremely uncertain. Rich people should remember that God gives them their riches, and He alone lets them enjoy those riches fully. Many people have riches but do not enjoy them well because they don’t know how to use them properly.

What’s the best estate worth if it doesn't give you chances to do more good? We should show our faith in Christ through actions of love. Let us grasp eternal life while the selfish, greedy, and godless around us suffer in torment.

Learning that goes against the truth of the gospel isn’t real knowledge. It wouldn’t support the gospel if it were true knowledge. People who put reason above faith risk losing faith.

Grace covers everything that is good. Grace is a down payment, a start toward glory. Wherever God gives grace, He will give glory too.
